name: Dutch Bread with Gouda

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the bread and top with the gouda.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread generously, top with the gouda, and finish with a little black pepper.
Bread with a thick slice of cheese, unmelted, is the standard Dutch breakfast and lunch, eaten daily without much variation.
description: Gouda, the Netherlands' most famous cheese, paired here simply with bread.

name: Dutch Oat Porridge with Honey
steps: Simmer the oats in the milk for 6-8 minutes, stirring, until thick.
Top with the honey.
steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes, until thick and creamy.
Top generously with the honey.
description: A simple breakfast porridge.

name: Dutch Eggs with Edam
steps: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter.
Fold in the edam just before they finish setting.
steps_detailed: Scramble the eggs gently in the butter over low heat, stirring often for soft curds.
Fold in the edam and pepper just before they finish setting, letting the cheese soften through.
description: Edam, a classic Dutch cheese, paired here with eggs.

name: Dutch Stamppot with Cabbage
steps: Simmer the cabbage until tender, about 12-15 minutes.
Mash the potato with the milk and butter, then mash the cabbage through it.
steps_detailed: Simmer the cabbage until fully tender, 14-16 minutes, then drain well.
Mash the potato with the milk, butter, and pepper until smooth, then mash the cabbage through it until well combined.
Stamppot, mashed potato worked through with a vegetable, is one of the most enduring Dutch comfort foods, especially in winter.
description: Stamppot, mashed potato with vegetables folded through, a quintessential Dutch comfort food.

name: Dutch Hutspot (Stamppot with Rutabaga)
steps: Simmer the rutabaga with the onion until tender, about 20 minutes.
Mash the potato with the butter, then mash the rutabaga and onion through it.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rutabaga with the onion until fully tender, 22-25 minutes, then drain well.
Mash the potato with the butter and pepper, then mash the rutabaga and onion through it until well combined.
description: Hutspot, one of the oldest recorded Dutch dishes, is traditionally tied to the 1574 liberation of Leiden.

name: Dutch Cheese Soup
steps: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, then the milk, and simmer 5 minutes.
Stir in the gouda until melted.
steps_detailed: Melt the butter, stir in the flour, and cook 1 minute, then whisk in the milk gradually, simmering 6-8 minutes until slightly thickened.
Stir in the gouda and pepper until fully melted and smooth.
description: A simple cheese soup, Dutch cheese being world-famous.

name: Dutch Pickled Herring Plate

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Serve the pickled herring with the onion and bread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread and serve alongside the pickled herring and onion.
description: Raw or lightly cured herring, eaten by the tail and dipped in onion, is a genuine Dutch classic, sold from street stalls across the country.

name: Dutch Meatballs (Gehaktballen)
steps: Mix the beef with the egg, flour, and onion, and form into balls.
Pan-fry in a little oil, 8-10 minutes, until browned through.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef thoroughly with the egg, flour, onion, and pepper, and form into large, even balls.
Pan-fry in the butter over medium heat, 9-11 minutes, turning, until deeply browned and cooked through.
Gehaktballen, dense meatballs served with gravy and potatoes, are a Dutch home-cooking staple.
description: Gehaktballen, Dutch meatballs, a home-cooking staple often served with gravy.

name: Dutch Stamppot with Beef Sausage
steps: Form the beef into sausage shapes and pan-fry until browned through.
Simmer the cabbage until tender, mash the potato with the butter, then mash the cabbage through it, and serve with the sausage.
steps_detailed: Season the beef with the pepper, form into sausage shapes, and pan-fry over medium heat, 10-12 minutes, until browned through.
Simmer the cabbage until fully tender, 14-16 minutes, mash the potato with the butter, then mash the cabbage through it, and serve alongside the sausage.
description: Stamppot with sausage, a hearty Dutch comfort food staple.

name: Dutch Baked Salmon with Dill
steps: Season the salmon with the dill and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until just cooked through. Finish with the lime juice.
steps_detailed: Season the salmon with the dill and pepper and dot with the butter.
Bake at 400F (200C) for 16-20 minutes until just cooked through and flaking easily.
description: Finish with the lime juice - the Netherlands has a long fishing and trading history built around the North Sea, and simply prepared fish reflects that.

name: Dutch Roast Pork with Apple

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Roast the pork at 400F for 25-28 minutes until browned.
Soften the apple and onion in the butter, and serve alongside.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Roast the pork at 400F (200C) for 26-30 minutes until well browned and the fat has rendered.
Soften the apple, onion, and cinnamon in the butter, 8-10 minutes, until tender and lightly caramelized, and serve alongside.
description: Roast pork with apple, a classic Dutch flavor pairing.

name: Dutch Bitterballen-Style Fried Beef Balls
steps: Form the beef into small balls and simmer until cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, and fry in the oil until golden.
steps_detailed: Form the beef, seasoned with the pepper, into small balls and simmer in water 9-11 minutes until cooked through.
Dredge in the flour, then the beaten egg, pressing to coat well, and fry in the oil over medium-high heat, 3-4 minutes, until deeply golden and crisp.
description: Bitterballen, traditionally a smooth ragout inside a crisp shell, are the quintessential Dutch bar snack.

name: Dutch Gouda and Apple Snack

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Arrange the gouda and apple slices together.
Drizzle with the honey.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Arrange the gouda and apple slices together, drizzle with the honey, and scatter with the walnuts.
description: Gouda and apple, a classic Dutch flavor pairing.

name: Dutch Edam and Bread Snack

ingredients.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps: Butter the bread and top with the edam.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Bimbo Classic Rye Organic Bread, Classic Rye

steps_detailed: Butter the bread generously, top with the edam, and finish with a little black pepper.
description: Edam and bread, a simple Dutch snack.

name: Dutch Rutabaga Fries
steps: Toss the rutabaga fries with the oil and pepper.
Roast at 425F for 25-30 minutes, turning once, until golden.
steps_detailed: Toss the rutabaga fries with the oil and pepper.
Roast at 425F (220C) for 26-32 minutes, turning once, until golden and crisp at the edges.
Serve with the mustard for dipping.
description: Simple rutabaga fries.

name: Dutch Stroopwafel-Style Honey Cookies
steps: Mix the flour, honey, butter, and cinnamon into a firm dough.
Roll thin, cut into rounds, and bake at 350F for 10-12 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, honey, butter, cinnamon, and sugar into a firm, smooth dough.
Roll thin, cut into rounds, and bake at 350F (175C) for 11-14 minutes until golden at the edges.
A true stroopwafel is two thin waffle layers filled with warm syrup - this keeps the same honey-cinnamon character in a simpler cookie form.
description: Stroopwafel, thin waffle cookies filled with caramel syrup, one of the Netherlands' most iconic treats.

name: Dutch Apple Cake (Appeltaart-Style)

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Toss the apple with half the sugar and the cinnamon.
Mix the flour, butter, and remaining sugar into a crumbly topping, layer with the apple in a small dish, and bake at 375F for 30-35 minutes until golden.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Toss the apple with half the sugar and the cinnamon.
Rub the butter into the flour and remaining sugar until crumbly, layer with the apple in a small baking dish, and bake at 375F (190C) for 32-38 minutes until golden and the apple is tender.
Appeltaart, thick with apple and lightly spiced, is considered a point of national pride in the Netherlands.
description: Appeltaart, Dutch apple cake, denser and more custard-like than American apple pie.

name: Dutch Rice Pudding
steps: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon and honey for 25-30 minutes until thick and creamy.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the milk with the cinnamon, honey, and butter over low heat, stirring often, 28-32 minutes, until thick and creamy.
description: A simple rice pudding.

name: Dutch Mustard Sauce
steps: Warm the mustard, butter, and milk together gently until smooth.
steps_detailed: Warm the mustard, butter, milk, and pepper together gently over low heat, stirring, until smooth.
Good with fried fish or bitterballen.
description: A simple mustard sauce.

name: Dutch Apple Compote

ingredients.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Simmer the apple with the honey, cinnamon, and a splash of water, 10-12 minutes, until it breaks down into a compote.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Apples, raw, granny smith, with skin (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Simmer the apple with the honey, cinnamon, and a splash of water over medium-low heat, 12-14 minutes, mashing as it softens, until it breaks down into a thick, glossy compote.
Applemoes, smooth stewed apple, is a common accompaniment to savory dishes across the Netherlands, not reserved for dessert.
description: A simple apple compote.
